title: penstemon crandallii var. procumbens
intro: Penstemon crandallii var. procumbens, also known as procumbent beardtongue, is a variety of the Penstemon crandallii plant, a flowering perennial native to the southwestern United States. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for treating respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Penstemon crandallii var. procumbens include reducing inflammation, relieving congestion, and soothing sore throats. Herbal preparations of this plant often involve making teas, infusions, or tinctures from its leaves and flowers to harness its medicinal properties.
